mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show positive mean when FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and recession risk, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE growth is below the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand strength, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce positive returns when either series declines, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which translates to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during tightening periods, or 5-day forward returns show no significant positive mean when INDPRO grows.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds a 7.0% annualized threshold, markets price in aggressive central bank tightening, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds the threshold, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market expansion, supporting corporate earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative returns when payrolls rise but unemployment also rises, or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from zero in regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product reflects underlying economic expansion that supports corporate profit growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show negative mean during positive GDP growth quarters, or Rank IC falls below 0.01 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals severe supply-side contraction and imminent economic slowdown, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >50% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction when industrial production declines moderately.
